title: [Feature]: Evaluate a Google Pub/Sub sink
problem: Oracle GoldenGate lists Google Pub/Sub as a target-only connection and Google Pub/Sub Kafka compatibility mode in the broader connectivity catalog. nats-sinks does not yet have a Google Pub/Sub sink.
proposal: Research a Google Pub/Sub sink that publishes normalized event envelopes with bounded batches, ordering-key options, idempotency metadata, attribute allow-listing, retry controls, and explicit durable acceptance semantics. The work should also decide whether Pub/Sub Kafka compatibility belongs under the Kafka connector certification matrix.
users: Google Cloud users delivering JetStream events into Pub/Sub-driven applications, Dataflow, Cloud Functions, or analytics pipelines.
delivery_semantics: ACK-gated success requires successful publish futures for every required message. Partial publish failures must prevent ACK for affected events.
security: Use workload identity or service-account credentials through secure runtime configuration. Topic names, attributes, payloads, ordering keys, and project identifiers must be redacted.
acceptance: Research documents Pub/Sub publish confirmation and ordering behavior.
The design handles partial failures and duplicate delivery.
Configuration validates topic, project, batching, timeout, and ordering fields.
Unit tests use fakes and make no network calls.
Documentation covers IAM, quotas, and replay behavior.
tests: Add mocked publisher tests for batching, publish future failures, attributes, ordering keys, and ACK ordering. Gate live tests.
documentation: Add Google Pub/Sub sink documentation and update roadmap if implemented.
closeout: Close after the research or implementation release is published.
